description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a reddish-brown coloration and scattered lighter patches across its rough surface. The stone has a somewhat angular profile, with one side appearing broader and flatter, while the opposite side tapers to a thinner edge. Its surface texture is coarse and matte, with visible natural pitting and small chips, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements. There are no visible carvings or artificial modifications, but a faint marking or number is present on one side, possibly for cataloging or identification. The stone's coloration and weathering indicate it could be sandstone or a similar sedimentary rock. Its size and mass make it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in urban parks, gardens, or along pathways.
description: A reddish-brown, rough-textured stone with irregular shape and lighter patches, showing signs of natural weathering and minor markings.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its rough, angular surface and reddish-brown color with a few lighter spots.
description_view_1: The stone appears much thinner from this side, emphasizing its narrow edge and elongated profile.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the underside and more of the stone's irregular, pitted surface, with additional lighter patches visible.
description_view_3: A side view showing the stone's tapering thickness and a visible marking or number on the surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with scattered lighter patches; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and naturally pitted, with minor chips and surface wear indicating moderate weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones; may contrast with paved surfaces or manicured lawns.
